title: eria sublobulata
intro: Eria sublobulata, a species of orchid native to Sri Lanka, is a type of flowering plant characterized by its unique, white or yellowish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eria sublobulata include its potential to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often combined with other medicinal herbs and used in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its therapeutic properties.
